evidence.reference_title: Hereditary angioedema with C1Inh deficiency (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0002027 | Abdominal pain | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.snippet: HP:0100665 | Angioedema | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Plasma-derived or recombinant C1-INH replacement restores the deficient regulator in C1-INH-deficiency HAE and can be used for long-term prophylaxis against attacks.

evidence.reference_title: Prevention of Hereditary Angioedema Attacks with a Subcutaneous C1 Inhibitor.
evidence.snippet: In patients with hereditary angioedema, the prophylactic use of a subcutaneous C1 inhibitor twice weekly significantly reduced the frequency of acute attacks.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary angioedema with C1Inh deficiency (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0034204 | Decreased circulating C1-esterase inhibitor concentration | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary angioedema with C1Inh deficiency (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0045042 | Decreased circulating complement C4 concentration | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary angioedema with C1Inh deficiency (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0002014 | Diarrhea | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Donidalorsen (Dawnzera) is a prekallikrein-directed antisense oligonucleotide approved for long-term prophylaxis of hereditary angioedema attacks. RNase H1-mediated knockdown of KLKB1 (prekallikrein) mRNA lowers plasma prekallikrein, reducing bradykinin generation upstream of edema attacks.

evidence.reference_title: Donidalorsen for Long-Term Prophylaxis of Hereditary Angioedema Attacks: Results from the OASISplus Open-Label Extension Cohort at Year 1.
evidence.snippet: Donidalorsen demonstrated sustained reductions in HAE attack rate, improvements in QoL, and an acceptable safety profile after 1 year of treatment.

evidence.reference_title: Inhibition of Prekallikrein for Hereditary Angioedema.
evidence.snippet: donidalorsen treatment resulted in a significantly lower rate of angioedema attacks than placebo in this small, phase 2 trial.

nodes.description: Ecallantide inhibits plasma kallikrein and thereby reduces bradykinin production during acute HAE attacks.

evidence.reference_title: Ecallantide (DX-88) for acute hereditary angioedema attacks: integrated analysis of 2 double-blind, phase 3 studies.
evidence.snippet: Compared with placebo, ecallantide resulted in significantly greater reduction in MSCS scores from baseline to 4 hours after dosing

evidence.snippet: HP:0010742 | Edema of the upper limbs | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary angioedema with C1Inh deficiency (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:6001012 | Erythema marginatum | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Excess bradykinin is the central mediator of HAE attacks, increasing vascular permeability in superficial tissues and gastrointestinal and respiratory mucosa.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary Angioedema: A Review.
evidence.snippet: excessive bradykinin production, with subsequent increased vascular permeability

evidence.reference_title: Icatibant, a new bradykinin-receptor antagonist, in hereditary angioedema.
evidence.snippet: Bradykinin is the key mediator of symptoms.

meta.association: Causative gene for estrogen-sensitive normal-C1-INH HAE

nodes.description: Icatibant is a bradykinin B2 receptor antagonist used as on-demand treatment for acute HAE attacks.

evidence.reference_title: Icatibant, a new bradykinin-receptor antagonist, in hereditary angioedema.
evidence.snippet: In FAST-2, the median time to clinically significant relief of the index symptom was 2.0 hours with icatibant versus 12.0 hours with tranexamic acid (P<0.001)

evidence.snippet: HP:0012027 | Laryngeal edema | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Long-term prophylaxis can target plasma kallikrein with agents such as lanadelumab and berotralstat to reduce attack frequency. Prekallikrein lowering with the antisense oligonucleotide donidalorsen is curated as a dedicated treatment below.

evidence.reference_title: Effect of Lanadelumab Compared With Placebo on Prevention of Hereditary Angioedema Attacks: A Randomized Clinical Trial.
evidence.snippet: treatment with subcutaneous lanadelumab for 26 weeks significantly reduced the attack rate compared with placebo.

evidence.reference_title: Randomized Trial of the Efficacy and Safety of Berotralstat (BCX7353) as an Oral Prophylactic Therapy for Hereditary Angioedema: Results of APeX-2 Through 48 Weeks (Part 2).
evidence.snippet: Berotralstat (BCX7353) is a recently approved, oral, once-daily kallikrein inhibitor for hereditary angioedema (HAE) prophylaxis.

nodes.description: Normal-C1-INH HAE includes genetically defined non-mast-cell angioedema families with F12, PLG, KNG1, ANGPT1, HS3ST6, MYOF, or other variants that converge on bradykinin-mediated or contact-system angioedema biology.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary Angioedema with Normal C1 Inhibitor: an Updated International Consensus Paper on Diagnosis, Pathophysiology, and Treatment.
evidence.snippet: proven genetic pathogenic variants that co-segregate with angioedema expression within families.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary Angioedema: Diagnosis, Clinical Implications, and Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: underlying mutations such as in kininogen-1 (HAE-KNG1), plasminogen gene (PLG-HAE), myoferlin gene mutation (MYOF-HAE), heparan sulfate-glucosamine 3-sulfotransferase 6 (HS3ST6), mutation in Hageman factor (factor XII), and in angiopoietin-1 (HAE-ANGPT-1).

nodes.description: SERPING1 pathogenic variants cause quantitative C1-INH deficiency in type 1 HAE or qualitative C1-INH dysfunction in type 2 HAE, reducing regulation of complement, coagulation, fibrinolytic, and contact-system cascades.
nodes.id: SERPING1 C1 inhibitor deficiency or dysfunction

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary Angioedema: Diagnosis, Clinical Implications, and Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: Type I and II HAE result from a mutation in the SERPING1 gene, which encodes C1-INH.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary Angioedema: Diagnosis, Clinical Implications, and Pathophysiology.
evidence.snippet: C1-INH is important in the coagulation complement, contact systems, and fibrinolysis.

nodes.description: Sebetralstat is an oral plasma kallikrein inhibitor for on-demand treatment of HAE attacks due to C1-INH deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Long-Term Safety and Effectiveness of Sebetralstat: Interim Analysis of KONFIDENT-S Open-label Extension.
evidence.snippet: No new safety signals were observed, and effectiveness for repeated attacks was consistent with the KONFIDENT trial results.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000988 | Skin rash | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Increased vascular permeability causes transitory, recurrent, nonpitting subcutaneous and submucosal edema attacks involving skin, abdomen, genital tissues, pharynx, and larynx.
nodes.id: Vascular permeability and recurrent edema attacks

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary angioedema with C1Inh deficiency (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: recurrent attacks of nonpitting edema of the skin, severe abdominal symptoms such as pain and swelling, and/or respiratory distress due to upper respiratory airways involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary angioedema with normal C1Inh (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: Patients present with prolonged attacks which last for approximately two to five days and may include nonpitting edema of the skin, severe abdominal symptoms such as pain and swelling, and/or respiratory distress due to upper respiratory airways involvement.
